message:
fix(sessions-api): handle direct Agent Mail MCP response formats

The Agent Mail MCP tools can return responses in two formats:
1. Wrapped in structuredContent (legacy/compatibility)
2. Direct properties at the root level (current MCP behavior)

Previously, parseEnsureProjectSlug() and extractMessageId() only checked
the structuredContent wrapper, causing failures when the MCP server
returned direct responses. This manifested as "could not resolve project
slug" errors during session actions like publish and compile.

Changes to parseEnsureProjectSlug():
- Added direct result.slug check before falling back to structuredContent
- Maintains backward compatibility with wrapped responses
- Order: direct slug → structuredContent.slug → content[0].text JSON parse

Changes to extractMessageId():
- Added direct result.deliveries check before structuredContent
- Extracts payload.id from first delivery entry
- Same fallback chain for compatibility

This fix ensures session actions (compile, publish, request_critique,
post_delta) work correctly regardless of which response format the
Agent Mail MCP server uses, improving reliability of the Brenner Loop
artifact compilation pipeline.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
